_README: Accuracy judged 0/1/2 per litmus/accuracy.py rubric. 2=correct+real mechanism, 1=correct but surface definition, 0=factual error/misleading. Judge for gpt and qwen_* columns = Claude (harness operator), which is NOT the model being judged, so no self-enhancement bias for those columns. Do NOT let Claude judge the claude_browser column (self-bias) — have a human or a non-Claude judge score those.

Why is the sky blue?.note: Rayleigh scattering + blue scatters more (shorter waves) + correct sunset reddening. Real mechanism.

How do plants make their own food?.note: Photosynthesis: chlorophyll catches sunlight, combines with air+water into food. Mechanism present ('air' stands in for CO2).

Why do we have day and night?.note: Earth's rotation, one spin per day, facing/away from sun. Correct mechanism.

What makes ice melt?.note: Heat energy -> molecules move faster -> break apart -> phase change. Real mechanism.

How do magnets work?.note: Poles attract/repel; iron has tiny pieces that line up (domains). Mechanism at grade level; leans slightly on naming 'magnetic force'.

Why do things fall to the ground?.note: Gravity pulls; bigger Earth -> more gravity (mass link is the accessible cause). Correct.

Where does a puddle go when it dries up?.note: Evaporation: sun speeds molecules, they escape to air, liquid->gas. Real mechanism.

Why do we have seasons?.note: Axial tilt changes sunlight per region — the CORRECT mechanism (avoids the distance misconception).

How do our lungs help us breathe?.note: Oxygen from air into blood, carried around body, CO2 breathed out. Gas-exchange mechanism present.

What makes a rainbow?.note: Sunlight = many colors; bends and splits in raindrops (dispersion) + viewing geometry. Real mechanism.

Why does the moon look like it changes s.note: Moon reflects sun; as it orbits we see different amounts of the lit side. Correct mechanism.

How do fish breathe underwater?.note: Gills extract dissolved oxygen from water into blood. Real mechanism.

Why is the sky blue?.note: Says the gases 'create a blue color' — wrong causation; never says blue scatters more. Mentions scattering but misrepresents the mechanism.

How do plants make their own food?.note: Photosynthesis: sunlight + water + carbon dioxide -> food. Correct, names the reactants (brief).

Why do we have day and night?.note: Earth's rotation on its axis; explicitly corrects the 'sun moves' misconception. Correct mechanism.

What makes ice melt?.note: ERROR: claims ice turns into water vapor then back to liquid (melting is solid->liquid). 'Hot cup in a freezer starts to warm up' is incoherent.

How do magnets work?.note: ERROR: 'magnetic fields they create are the same, so they stick' — like poles repel, not attract. Attraction/repulsion described backwards.

Why do things fall to the ground?.note: Correct that gravity pulls down, but no mechanism (no mass link) and 'keeps them in the air until they hit the ground' is confused.

Where does a puddle go when it dries up?.note: Names evaporation but no mechanism; 'turns back into air' is loose (becomes water vapor, not air).

Why do we have seasons?.note: Correct tilt mechanism (differential sunlight). Final 'wheel that changes direction' analogy is misleading but the core mechanism is stated correctly.

How do our lungs help us breathe?.note: Conveys gas exchange (O2 into blood, CO2 out). Imprecise: attributes exchange to bronchioles rather than alveoli.

What makes a rainbow?.note: Sunlight bends/splits in droplets by wavelength (dispersion). Correct mechanism.

Why does the moon look like it changes s.note: ERROR: attributes phases to the moon 'rotating on its own'/spinning. Phases are from the sun lighting different parts as it ORBITS Earth.

How do fish breathe underwater?.note: ERROR: 'works because the water is warm and moist' misrepresents the mechanism (warm water holds LESS oxygen); says gills exchange with 'the air'.

Why is the sky blue?.note: Short (blue) waves scatter/bounce more than long (red) — the real differential-scattering mechanism, correctly explained.

How do plants make their own food?.note: Roots take water, leaves take in CO2 ('special gas'), sunlight combines them into sugar (food). Correct mechanism.

Why do we have day and night?.note: Earth spins; side facing sun = day, turned away = night. Correct mechanism.

What makes ice melt?.note: Heat energy makes locked pieces wiggle until they break free and slide (solid->liquid). Correct mechanism.

How do magnets work?.note: Aligned electron spins in a magnet (vs random/cancelling elsewhere) create the pulling force. Real mechanism at grade level.

Why do things fall to the ground?.note: Mass-based gravity, Earth pulls to center, apple pulls back but Earth too massive to move (Newton's 3rd law). Correct mechanism.

Where does a puddle go when it dries up?.note: Heat makes water pieces break apart and rise as invisible gas (evaporation), then clouds/rain. Correct mechanism.

Why do we have seasons?.note: Axial tilt -> directness of sunlight (direct=stronger=summer, slanted=weaker=winter). Full correct mechanism (not distance).

How do our lungs help us breathe?.note: Diaphragm pulls down -> space -> air sucked in; oxygen to blood, CO2 waste out. Correct mechanism incl. the muscle.

What makes a rainbow?.note: Refraction bends/splits colors (some bend more) + reflection off droplet back. Correct dispersion mechanism.

Why does the moon look like it changes s.note: Moon reflects sun; as it orbits we see different amounts of the lit side. Correct mechanism.

How do fish breathe underwater?.note: Dissolved oxygen in water; gills catch it into the blood as water flows over them. Correct mechanism.

Why is the sky blue?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes Rayleigh scattering, where blue light is scattered more than other colors due to its shorter wavelength.

How do plants make their own food?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation accurately describes photosynthesis, including the role of chlorophyll, sunlight, water, carbon dioxide, and the production of sugar and oxygen.

Why do we have day and night?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes Earth's rotation as the cause of day and night, including the mechanism of facing towards or away from the Sun.

What makes ice melt?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes how heat increases the energy of water molecules, causing them to move faster and transition from solid to liquid.

How do magnets work?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes the role of electron spin in creating a magnetic field and the interaction of magnetic poles.

Why do things fall to the ground?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes gravity as a force that pulls objects toward the Earth and explains the mechanism of Earth's gravity being stronger due to its larger mass.

Where does a puddle go when it dries up?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes evaporation and the role of the sun and wind, and it also explains the water cycle by mentioning cloud formation and rain.

Why do we have seasons?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes Earth's axial tilt and its effect on the angle of sunlight, explaining the mechanism behind seasonal changes.

How do our lungs help us breathe?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes the process of gas exchange in the lungs, including oxygen intake and carbon dioxide expulsion, and explains the role of blood vessels in transporting gases.

What makes a rainbow?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation accurately describes the mechanism of light refraction, reflection, and dispersion in raindrops that creates a rainbow.

Why does the moon look like it changes s.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes the moon's phases as a result of its orbit around Earth and the changing angles of sunlight, providing a clear analogy with a ball and lamp.

How do fish breathe underwater?.note: [GPT-4o judge] The explanation correctly describes the mechanism of how fish use gills to extract oxygen from water and transfer it into their bloodstream.
